skill.name: runcomfy-cli
skill.description: Run any model on RunComfy from the command line. The `runcomfy` CLI is one binary, one auth, hundreds of model endpoints — image generation, image edit, video generation, image-to-video, lip-sync, face swap, video edit, inpainting, outpainting, extend, ControlNet, relight, upscale, LoRA training and more. Submit a request, poll for status, download the output. This skill teaches the agent how to install, authenticate, discover model schemas, invoke models, stream / poll / no-wait, script in JSON output mode, and handle errors. Triggers on "runcomfy cli", "install runcomfy", "runcomfy login", "runcomfy run", "runcomfy whoami", "runcomfy api", or any explicit ask to call a RunComfy model from a script or terminal. Sibling skills (ai-image-generation, ai-video-generation, image-edit, video-edit, face-swap, lipsync, image-to-video, image-inpainting, image-outpainting, video-extend, controlnet-pose, relight) all dispatch through this CLI.
skill.summary: Command-line interface for RunComfy AI models including image, video, and audio generation.
skill.author: runcomfy-com

security_audit.summary: After AI review, all static findings are FALSE POSITIVES. The skill is documentation (SKILL.md) describing a CLI tool, not executable code. Shell commands and URLs flagged by the static analyzer are legitimate usage examples and service endpoints documented for user guidance. The skill explicitly restricts allowed-tools to `Bash(runcomfy *)` only and includes detailed security documentation covering shell injection prevention, token storage, and input boundary considerations.

low_findings.title: Documentation Contains Shell Command Examples
low_findings.description: SKILL.md contains examples of shell commands (npm install, runcomfy run, curl). These are legitimate documentation examples showing users how to use the CLI tool, not actual code execution vulnerabilities.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill is documentation-only, not executable code. All shell commands are usage examples for the legitimate runcomfy CLI tool.

low_findings.title: Documentation Contains URLs to RunComfy Services
low_findings.description: SKILL.md documents URLs to runcomfy.com, docs.runcomfy.com, and api.runcomfy.net. These are legitimate service endpoints for the RunComfy AI platform.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All URLs are legitimate RunComfy service endpoints described in official documentation.

low_findings.title: Documentation References Configuration File Path
low_findings.description: SKILL.md mentions ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json for storing the API token. This is legitimate token storage as documented by the CLI tool.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Token storage at ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json with mode 0600 is explicitly documented security practice.

low_findings.title: Documentation Mentions System Commands
low_findings.description: SKILL.md includes examples of system commands like runcomfy --version and runcomfy whoami. These are standard CLI usage patterns.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: runcomfy --version, runcomfy whoami are standard CLI verification commands - true positives (legitimate usage).

content.user_title: Call AI generation models from Claude Code
content.value_statement: Access hundreds of AI generation models (images, video, audio) from the command line through a unified CLI interface without writing API integration code.
actual_capabilities: Install and authenticate with the runcomfy CLI tool
Run text-to-image models (FLUX, GPT Image 2, Stable Diffusion, etc.)
Run video generation models (Veo, Kling, Wan, Seedance, etc.)
Run audio/lip-sync models for video avatar generation
Poll job status and download generated outputs
Script batch processing with JSON output mode
limitations: Does not include model API keys - requires separate RunComfy account
Cannot generate images, video, or audio directly - only orchestrates the runcomfy CLI
Output file size capped at 2 GiB by the CLI (prevents disk-fill attacks)

use_cases.title: Generate AI images from the terminal
use_cases.description: Use a text prompt to generate images with state-of-the-art models without writing API code.
use_cases.target_user: Developers and designers who want quick AI image generation

use_cases.title: Batch process multiple generation requests
use_cases.description: Script multiple model invocations from a file of prompts with automatic retry logic.

use_cases.title: Create AI-generated video content
use_cases.description: Generate video from text prompts or animate existing images using video models.

prompt_templates.title: Install and sign in
prompt_templates.prompt: Install the runcomfy CLI and authenticate with my RunComfy account
prompt_templates.scenario: Getting started with the CLI tool

prompt_templates.title: Generate an image
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an image of a purple cat at sunset using the runcomfy CLI

prompt_templates.title: Run a specific model
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the Flux 2 model to generate a photorealistic image of mountains

prompt_templates.title: Batch process from file
prompt_templates.prompt: Process all prompts from prompts.txt using the runcomfy CLI and save outputs to ./results
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced batch processing with custom directory

output_examples.input: Generate an image of a sunset over mountains
output_examples.output: The CLI submits the request, polls until completion, and downloads the generated image to the current directory: ./result.png
The CLI returns your email, token type, and user ID to confirm authentication is working

best_practices: Store your RUNCOMFY_TOKEN in an environment variable for CI/CD usage instead of interactive login
Use --no-wait and poll separately for long-running jobs to avoid blocking your terminal
Set --timeout explicitly for batch jobs to prevent indefinite waiting on slow models
anti_patterns: Do not pipe install scripts directly into bash - review the script content first
Do not log or echo your API token into prompts or commit it to version control
Do not ingest URLs from untrusted sources as model inputs - this can enable indirect prompt injection

faq.question: What is runcomfy?
faq.answer: RunComfy is a platform that provides access to hundreds of AI generation models through a unified API and CLI interface.

faq.question: Do I need an API key?
faq.answer: Yes, you need a RunComfy account. Sign up at runcomfy.com and use runcomfy login or set the RUNCOMFY_TOKEN environment variable.

faq.question: What models are available?
faq.answer: The platform includes models for image generation, video generation, lip-sync, face swap, image editing, inpainting, and more. Run runcomfy models to browse the full catalog.

faq.question: Can I use this with Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill is designed for use with Claude Code and other Claude agent tools. It uses allowed-tools to restrict commands to runcomfy subcommands only.

faq.question: Is my data safe?
faq.answer: The CLI only sends requests to RunComfy endpoints. Your token is stored locally with mode 0600 permissions and is never transmitted to third parties.

faq.question: What exit codes does the CLI return?
faq.answer: Exit code 0 means success. Codes 64-77 indicate specific errors: bad args (64), schema mismatch (65), server errors (69), retryable timeout (75), or auth failure (77).
